content: pyvirtualcam on Windows supports two backends: 'obs' (requires OBS 26+ installed) and 'unitycapture' (requires Unity Capture installation). Both require external software. CRITICAL: OpenCV's VideoCapture via DirectShow cannot read OBS Virtual Camera — returns blank/black frames (OpenCV issue #19746, OBS issue #3635). Third-party obs-virtual-cam plugin is the only workaround, which is additional external software. The internal loop pattern (pyvirtualcam write + OpenCV read from virtual cam) is NOT viable on Windows due to the DirectShow blank-frame bug. Unity Capture could work but requires installation. The root cause of OBS eliminating MJPEG lag is OBS using hardware-accelerated Media Foundation decode, NOT the virtual camera intermediary. Replicating OBS's decode path in Python is not feasible without additional system-level libraries.
